stemHtml:
While flying in the NAT OTS (North Atlantic Organised Track System), you need to deviate to avoid weather by 4 NM from the track centreline. You can not get clearance from ATC. Which of the following options should you do? 

1. Look out for other aircraft both visually and on ACAS 
2. Declare an emergency on 121.500 MHz and inform other pilots on 123.450 MHz 
3. Inform other nearby aircraft on 121.500 MHz 
4. If possible deviate away from the track 
5. Climb or descend by 300 ft 
6. Ensure all aircraft lights are on
